mnemonics: S.M.A.R.T. Cores: Many Simple Cores, Accelerating Repeated Tasks concurrently.
keyPoints: GPUs use massive parallelism with thousands of smaller, efficient cores (compared to CPU's few complex cores).
Memory bandwidth and latency are critical performance factors; GPUs use high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and caching strategies to mitigate latency.
Optimal performance requires maximizing occupancy (active warps/warps per SM) and efficient memory access patterns (coalescing).
commonMistakes: Assuming parallelism (more threads) automatically equals better performance, without considering overhead and coordination costs (Amdahl's Law).
Using inefficient memory access patterns that are not coalesced, leading to high latency and poor bandwidth utilization.
Not considering the high cost of data transfer between CPU (host) and GPU (device) memory, which can become a bottleneck.
